1: This is the Lord 's message, which he gave to Cushi's son, Zephaniah. Cushi was the son of Gedaliah. Gedaliah was the son of Amariah, and Amariah was the son of Hezekiah. The Lord gave this message to Zephaniah when Josiah ruled Judah as king. Josiah was the son of Amon, king of Judah.
The Lord says, ‘I will destroy everything that is on the earth.

I will destroy people and animals. I will destroy the birds in the sky. and the fish in the sea. I will remove all the wicked people who cause trouble. Yes, I will remove all the people who live on the earth. No one will remain.’ That is what the Lord says.

‘I will attack Judah and the people who live in Jerusalem. I will remove from these places everyone who serves the false god Baal. I will remove all Baal's priests. Nobody will remember them any more.

I will destroy the people who worship the sun, the moon and the stars. They stand on their roofs to do that. Some people promise to be faithful to me, but they also worship their god Molech. So I will destroy those people too.
I will destroy those people who have turned away from me, the Lord. They do not ask me for help or advice.’
Be quiet and listen to the Lord God! The day of the will soon come. The Lord is ready to offer Judah's people as a sacrifice. He has chosen their enemies to do this.
The Lord says, ‘When the day comes for me to offer that sacrifice, I will punish Judah's leaders and the king's sons. I will punish everyone who wears the clothes of foreign people.
At that time, I will also punish everyone who worships foreign gods. I will punish those who rob other people to fill their master's house with valuable things. They are cruel people who tell lies.’

The Lord says, ‘On that day, you will hear people at the Fish Gate. They will be shouting because they are afraid. People will be crying loudly in the new part of Jerusalem. There will be loud noises in the hills around the city.
Weep, you people who live near Jerusalem's market! All the traders will disappear. There will be no people who weigh silver coins for you.

At that time, I will use lamps to find people in Jerusalem who are hiding in dark places. I will punish people who do not think that their sins are important. They think, “The Lord will not do anything to us, either good things or bad things.”

But their enemies will take away their valuable things. The houses of those people will become a heap of stones. If they build houses for themselves, they will never live in them. If they plant vineyards to grow grapes, they will never drink any wine from those grapes.’
The great day of the will soon be here. It will come very quickly. That day will bring terrible trouble. Brave soldiers will cry loudly in the battle.

It will be a day when God shows his great anger. People will be very upset because of the trouble that comes to them. Enemies will destroy many things. The day will be very dark, and black clouds will cover the sky.
There will be the loud noise of trumpets. Soldiers will shout as they go to fight. The walls of cities will fall in the battle. The strong high towers will fall down too.

The Lord says, ‘I will cause people to suffer because they have not obeyed me. They will walk around like blind people. Blood will pour out of their bodies like dust. Their dead bodies will lie on the ground like rubbish.’

On that day when the Lord shows his anger, their money will not save them. His anger will destroy the whole earth, as fire completely destroys things. He will quickly destroy all the people who live on the earth. It will be a day of terrible trouble.
2: Come together! Yes, come together now! You are a nation of people who should be ashamed of yourselves.

The Lord has decided the day for your punishment, so come together before it happens. It will happen very quickly, as wind blows away chaff. The Lord 's great anger will suddenly hit you. Do something before that day arrives!

Turn to the Lord, all you humble people of Judah who obey his commands. Do things that are good and right and be humble. Then perhaps the Lord will protect you from his punishment, on the day of his great anger.
No people will remain in Gaza. Ashkelon will become a heap of stones. Enemies will chase out the people of Ashdod in one morning. They will also destroy Ekron.

Terrible trouble will come to you people who came from Crete, you people who live beside the sea! The Lord has spoken against you, Philistines who live in the land of Canaan! He says, ‘I will destroy everyone who lives there!’
Your land beside the sea will become fields where shepherds feed their sheep. They will have safe places there for their sheep and goats.

The land will belong to the people of Judah's tribe who are still alive. They will live safely there. They will use the houses in Ashkelon as places to rest in the evening. The Lord, their God, will take care of them. He will give them success again.

The Lord says, ‘I have heard the people of Moab and the people of Ammon. They have insulted my people and they have laughed at them. They have boasted that they would attack my people's land and take it for themselves.’

The Lord Almighty, Israel's God, says, ‘I promise this, as surely as I live. I will cause Moab and Ammon to become like the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah. Weeds will cover the land. People will dig salt out of the ground. It will become a wilderness for ever. My people who are still alive will take the land for themselves. They will keep all the valuable things.’
God will punish the people of Moab and Ammon as they deserve. They have been very proud people. They have laughed at the people who belong to the Lord Almighty.

The Lord will cause them to be very afraid. He will destroy the power of all the earth's false gods. The people of all nations will worship the Lord. They will worship him in the places where they live.
The Lord will also punish you people of Ethiopia. He will cause you to die in battle.

The Lord will attack the people who live in the north. He will destroy the nation of Assyria. He will destroy its capital city, Nineveh. It will become a heap of stones where nobody lives. The land will be as dry as a desert.

Sheep, goats and cows will lie down there, as well as many wild animals. Hawks and owls will sleep on top of the pillars. They will call through the windows. Stones will lie on the ground in front the buildings. Cedar beams will lie outside.

All this will happen to that proud city, the city where people were happy and safe. They thought, ‘We live in the best city of all! No other city is as great as our city!’ But now that city will become a heap of stones. Only wild animals will have their homes there. Everyone who sees that place will laugh at it. They will shake their fists because it has lost its power.
3: Terrible trouble will come to this city! It is a dirty, wicked place, full of cruel people.
The people of the city do not obey any laws. They do not listen to advice. They do not trust in the Lord. They do not ask their God to help them.
The city's rulers roar like lions to make people afraid. Its judges are like hungry wolves. They kill their food in the evening and by the next morning nothing remains.
The city's prophets are proud people that nobody can trust. Its priests do not obey God's laws. They cause God's temple to be an unclean place.
But the Lord is in the city and he never does any wrong thing. Every day he shows that he is fair. He never fails to do what is right. But wicked people are not ashamed when they do bad things.

The Lord says, ‘I have destroyed nations. I have knocked down the strong walls and towers of their cities. I have caused their streets to be empty, with no people in them. Nobody lives in their cities any more.

I thought, “Now you people of Jerusalem will respect me! Now you will listen to me when I warn you.” If my people had obeyed me, I would not have destroyed their city. I would not punish them as they deserve. But they all continued to do evil things. Every day they only wanted to do something bad.

So you must wait for me to act. Wait for the day when I will show who is right. I have decided to bring together nations and kingdoms. Then I will punish them with my great anger. I will be so angry that I destroy the whole earth, like a fire that destroys everything.’ That is what the Lord says.
‘At that time, I will change people so that they speak words that are pure. Then they can pray to me, and they can all worship me together.
My people who now live far away, beyond the rivers of Ethiopia, will bring offerings to me.

At that time, you people of Jerusalem will not be ashamed, because you will no longer turn against me. I will remove from among you those people who boast about themselves. There will be no proud people who remain on my holy hill.
But I will leave a group of poor and humble people among you. They will trust me, the Lord, to keep them safe.
The Israelites who remain will be honest people. They will not speak lies. They will not deceive other people. They will have food to eat and a place to sleep. Nobody will cause them to be afraid.’
Be happy and sing, you people of Zion! Yes, shout aloud, you people of Israel! People of Jerusalem, use everything in you to show how happy you are!
The Lord has taken away the punishment that you deserved. He has sent your enemy's army away. The Lord, Israel's King, is with you! You do not need to be afraid of trouble any more.
On that day, people will say to Jerusalem, ‘Do not be afraid, people of Zion! Do not let fear make you weak!

The Lord, your God, is with you. He is strong, like a brave soldier. He will save you. He will be very happy because he is pleased with you! He will give you new life because of his love. He will sing with joy because you make him happy!’

The Lord says, ‘I will bring together those people who were sad because they could not meet together at the festivals. I will take away the trouble that you have had, and you will no longer feel ashamed.

Listen to me! At that time, I will punish all those people who have been cruel to you. I will rescue those who are helpless. I will bring back together the people who have gone away. I will cause people to give them honour so they will no longer be ashamed. People everywhere in the world will praise them.

At that time, I will bring you back together and I will lead you home. Yes, I will cause all the nations of the earth to respect you. You will have success again, and you will see me do this.’ That is what the Lord says.
